top_signals.title: Markets Brace for CPI, Warsh Hearing & Bank Earnings | Bloomberg Brief 07/14/2026
top_signals.summary: Brent climbs above $87 a barrel after President Trump reimposed the naval blockade of Iranian ships. Markets await the latest US inflation data ahead of Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh's testimony before Congress. Plus, five of the six largest US lenders report earnings before the opening bell. Abigail Watt of UBS previews CPI and Chair Warsh's testimony. Saul Martinez of HSBC looks ahead to bank earnings. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US CPI Falls for the First Time Since 2020, Core Gauge Flat
top_signals.summary: US consumer prices declined in June for the first time in six years and a key gauge of underlying inflation was little changed, taking some pressure off the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.
